ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: Ʋ gyɩ aa bɩɩnɩ gɛɛ tɩyaŋ nɛ, malɩka ko ʋ lee duwoso tɩyaŋ a basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Gyosɛfʋ, Kuwori Deviti doho nal, ta leŋ dɩ kambɩŋ kana ɩ, ɩ aa sɩ kpa Mɛɛrɩ a yaa ɩ haaŋ, beewɩya Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ dee rɛ tɩŋ ʋ kaŋ biye no luwol.
translated: While he was thinking about this, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, "Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because what is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
text: Ma kɩ yaa wɩya hʋ aa daga anɩɩ ma bɩrɩma lɩɩ ma wɩbɔmɔ tɩyaŋ nɛ,
translated: If you don't want him to beat you again, don't play with my things,

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Nyɔgɩmaa pʋna gannɩ rɛ Gyɔɔŋ gyɩ fa kɩ laalɩ, aŋ aa kpa tɛpɩɩŋbii vʋwa ʋ tɩŋa. Nyɩŋgɩrɩgɩsɩ abee tʋʋ rɛ gyɩ yaa ʋ kɩdiiliye.
translated: The woman who gave birth yesterday to Gyɔɔŋ is not well, and she has developed a fever in her body. The doctor said that she should rest.

ref: Many Pharisees and Sadducees also came to be baptized. But John said to them: You bunch of snakes! Who warned you to run from the coming judgment?
text: Amɛ Gyɔɔŋ aa ko na dɩ Farasiima abee Sadusiima yʋga aa kɩ ko ʋ lee dɩ ʋ fo ba wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ gɛɛ, ɛɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ɛɛdɔŋ dʋma! Ma dawarɩ yʋga ma tɩyaŋ! Ma bɩɩna ma sɩ wuwo fá nyʋgɩsɩ kyɛɛ hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa sɩ di nala sarɩya rɛ koo?
translated: But when Jesus saw that the Pharisees and Sadducees were coming to him for baptism, he said to them, "You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the coming wrath?"

ref: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
text: Ʋ kol ʋ mɩkyaarɩgbaha rɛ, dɩ ʋ ko ŋmaa ʋ mɩya buloŋ teŋ, dɩ ʋ kpa gbaha hʋ a kyaarɩ lɩɩ miŋbii hʋ a we ʋ busol tɩyaŋ aŋ kpa mɩŋgyɔrʋ hʋ we diŋ tɩyaŋ. Diŋ hʋ aa bɩ sɩ maakyiye dɩsɩ.” Gyɔɔŋ foo Yesu wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ Maakɩ
translated: When you see the abomination of desolation standing where it should not be (let the reader understand), then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains, and let the one who is on the housetop not come down or enter to take anything out of his house, and let the one who is in the field not turn back to take his cloak. But woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing infants in those days." From the Gospel of Jesus according to Mark

ref: “Listen, lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, lands along the road to the sea and across the Jordan. Listen Galilee, land of the Gentiles!
text: Ʋ gyɩ baa, “Ma nala hʋ aa we Zebulɔŋ abee Nafitali paalʋʋ aa kyaasɩ Galili fuwobal hʋ, Gyɔɔdaŋ fuwobal hʋ kyolo, abee Galili paalʋʋ nala, lee hʋ tɩyaŋ nala hʋ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma aa yʋga, ma nɩɩ daha,
translated: He said, "The people living in the land of Zebulun and Naphtali, toward the Sea of Galilee, beyond the Jordan, and in Galilee of the Gentiles, the people who sat in darkness have seen a great light,"

ref: While Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw two brothers. One was Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting their net into the lake.
text: Yesu gyɩ kpaa Galili fuwobal hʋ nyʋwa a kpala kɩ baŋ dɩ ʋ naa fuwonaŋkasɩla balɩya dɩ ba aa kasɩ fuwonamɩɩsɩ abee ba keliŋsi. Ba gyɩ yaa nɩmmabalɩya rɛ. Kɩdɩgɩ feŋ nɛ yaa Siimɔŋ Ʋ naabiye hʋ mɛ feŋ nɛ Aŋduru.
translated: Jesus was walking by the Sea of Galilee when he saw two brothers who were fishermen casting their nets into the sea. They were fishermen. One was called Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew.

ref: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
text: Dɩ ɩ rɛ kaŋ kɩŋ, dɩ nal sʋla, kpa tɩya ʋ. Dɩ nal lɛ ko ɩ lee a kɩ kyɛ kɩŋ dɩ ʋ kyɩma, dɩ ɩ rɛ kana, kpa kyɩma ʋ. Yesu baa á kyo á dɔmɔ Luka
translated: If he comes at midnight, or even near dawn, and finds them ready, those servants will be blessed. The Gospel of Jesus Christ according to Luke

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: Dɩ ma ko kɩ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ, ma ta kɩ basɩ wɩgyamaa aa bɩ kaŋ memii, anɩɩ nala hʋ aa bɩ gyɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ aa kɩ yaa gɛɛ. Ba bɩɩna anɩɩ ba wɩbasɩgyamaa no rɛ sɩ leŋ dɩ Wɩɩsɩ nɩɩ ba kyʋwala.
translated: If we say that we have fellowship with God, and we walk in darkness, then we are lying and not doing the truth. But if we walk in the light as he is in the light, then we have fellowship with one another.

ref: Don't be like them. Your Father knows what you need even before you ask.
text: Ma ta kɩɩ ba. Beewɩya ma Kuwo kɛ gyɩŋ kɩŋ hʋ ma aa kyɛ rɛ, dɩ ma ha gba bɩ sʋla ʋ.
translated: I will go there. Because God has given me life and I am still alive, so I will go and greet him.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: Ɛɛwɩya, ma ta leŋ dɩ kyiye wɩya kyɩl ma. Kyɛɛ buloŋ kaŋ ʋ wɩgyʋʋlɩya rɛ. Ma ta bɩl kpaa wɩdʋma buloŋ pɛ kyɛɛ kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ bee ʋ wɩgyʋʋlɩya tɩyaŋ.”
translated: Yes, I will do what you have said. Because the matter is very important. I will not forget the matter or let it be lost.

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: Ɛɛwɩya ma ta leŋ kɩdiiliye hʋ ma aa sɩ di, koo kɩŋ hʋ ma aa sɩ nyʋwa, koo kɩŋ hʋ mɛ ma aa sɩ laalɩ wɩya kyɩl ma.
translated: If you do not want to eat it, or if you do not want to drink it, or if you do not want to take the thing home.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Ma deŋ na digibiisi, ba bee pala, ba bɩ kaŋ busolo mɛ a kɩ we kɩdiiliye, amɛ á Kuwo Wɩɩsɩ aa we wɩɩsɩbee tɩyaŋ nɛ aa tɩya ba kɩdiiliye. Ma yaŋ daa kaŋ tɔnɔ a te digibiisi no?
translated: When we sin, they don't punish us, they don't do to us what we deserve for our sins, because the Lord has removed our sins far away and has taken away our sins. How can we not praise him in return?

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Nala hʋ aa bɩ gyɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ dʋnɩya no tɩyaŋ, ba aa rɛ aa leŋ kɩna no wɩya kyɩl ba. Ma Kuwo aa we wɩɩsɩbee tɩyaŋ gyɩma anɩɩ ma maga dɩ ma kaŋ kɩna no buloŋ nɛ.
translated: For God so loved the world that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believes in him should not perish but have everlasting life.

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
text: Ɛɛ rɛ ɩ sɩ yaa basɩ tɩya ɩ dɔŋtɩɩna a baa dɩ ʋ leŋ dɩ ɩ lɩɩ gyagɩŋ hʋ aa we ʋ sɩɩ tɩyaŋ ta, aŋ bɩ sɩ wuwo lɩɩ daakuu hʋ aa we ɩ tɩɩ sɩɩ tɩyaŋ kɛ.
translated: If you do not tell your friend that what he is doing is not good in his eyes so that he can change, then you too are doing something that is not good in God's eyes.

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Ɛɛdɔŋ minaafigi tɩɩna! Laa sɩya lɩɩ daakuu hʋ aa we ɩ tɩɩ sɩɩ tɩyaŋ dɩ ɩ sɩya wuwo kɩ na weliŋ, dɩ ɩ na wuwo lɩɩ gyagɩŋ hʋ aa we ɩ dɔŋtɩɩna sɩɩ tɩyaŋ.
translated: Do not forget my teaching! Let your heart keep my commands because they will prolong your days and bring you peace, and they will add years of life to you.

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Tɩya hʋ aa kaŋ laaŋfɩya bɩ sɩ wuwo nɔŋ nɔŋbɔŋlɔrɩnɩ, tɩya hʋ mɛ aa bɩ kaŋ laaŋfɩya bɩ sɩ wuwo nɔŋ nɔŋweliye.
translated: The day he went to the farm with his younger brother, that day he also went to the farm with his older brother.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Ŋ sɩ ko leŋ dɩ ʋ deye.”
translated: And Jesus said to him, "I will come and heal him."

ref: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu sii kpa mʋ Gadara paalʋʋ fuwo hʋ kyolo. Dɩ baala balɩya lɩɩ bilni tɩyaŋ a ko kyeme ʋ. Gyɩŋbɔmɔ rɛ gyɩ kaŋ baala no ba kɩ yaarɩ. Nal buloŋ gyɩ bɩ wuwo kɩ tɩŋ ŋmanɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ baala no balɩya aa we, beewɩya, ba gyɩ bɔmɔ rɛ weliŋ.
translated: When Jesus arrived at the region of the Gadarenes, two demon-possessed men came out from the tombs to meet him. They were so fierce that no one could pass by that way. It had been a long time since anyone could travel on that road because these demon-possessed men were there, and they were very fierce.

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Matthew's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners were also there.
text: Nyɛ rɛ Yesu bee ʋ hatɩnna hʋ buloŋ kpa mʋ Matiwu dɩya, a hɔŋ kɩ di kɩna. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ lampolaala bee wɩbɔŋyaala mɛ ko pɛ ba tɩyaŋ, ba buloŋ kɩ di kɩna hʋ.
translated: When Jesus went to Matthew's house to eat, many tax collectors and sinners also came and ate with them.

ref: After Jesus had forced the demon out, the man started talking. The crowds were so amazed they began saying, “Nothing like this has ever happened in Israel!”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu kile gyɩŋbɔmɔ hʋ baal hʋ tɩyaŋ, ka baal hʋ piili kɩ basɩ wɩya. Ʋ yaa nal buloŋ wɩkperii. Ɛɛ rɛ ba baa, “Á ha bɩ maakyiye na wɩɩ no iriŋ Iziral paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ.”
translated: When Jesus saw the crowds, he had compassion on them, because they were harassed and helpless. He said to his disciples, "The harvest is plentiful but the workers are few. Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into his harvest field."

ref: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ bɩl maga namaga tɩya ba a baa, “Nal buloŋ bɩ sɩ kpa taŋfalɩɩ a ala tɔ gabɩnɩɩ bol. Dɩ ɩ rɛ yaa gɛɛ, taŋfalɩɩ hʋ sɩ kʋʋrɩ, dɩ gabɩnɩɩ hʋ marɩ kɩyasɩ pɛ.
translated: And when he had finished speaking, they said to him, "Lord, increase our faith so that we may be able to do these things. For if it is like this, faith is very difficult, and these things are not easy at all."

ref: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ma ta kɩ fáá nala hʋ aa sɩ wuwo kpʋ ma dʋŋ. Amɛ ma kɩ fá Wɩɩsɩ, beewɩya ʋ rɛ kaŋ dee ʋ aa sɩ wuwo leŋ dɩ ɩ sʋba, a kaŋ dee mɛ ʋ aa sɩ maakyiye kpa ɩ we diŋ.
translated: And Jesus said to them, "I did not come down so that people would do my will. But I came down from Heaven, so that I would do the will of the one who sent me, and complete the work that he gave me to do."

ref: Your worst enemies will be in your own family.
text: Nal dɩya nala tɩɩ rɛ sɩ yaa ʋ dɔmɔ, a haasɩ ʋ te nal buloŋ.
translated: If a person does something that you don't like, you should tell him nicely.

ref: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
text: ba mʋ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Basɩ tɩya ma, ɩ rɛ yaa nal hʋ Gyɔɔŋ fa aa baa dɩ ʋ sɩ ko laa ma ta hʋ, koo á ha kɩ gyegili nɩdɩma?”
translated: And when he asked him, he said, "My name is Legion, for we are many."

ref: “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not mourn!”
text: ‘Á yɩɩ yɩɩla rɛ tɩya ma, ka ma vɩya gʋwayɩ. Á taa haŋyee mɛ tɩya ma ka ma vɩya wiyi.”’
translated: "If you do good to me, I will do good to you. You should not do bad to me so I will do bad to you."

ref: So I tell you on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than you. Come to Me and Rest (Luke
text: Amɛ ŋ yaŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma rɛ, sarɩya hʋ kyɛdiilii Wɩɩsɩ sɩ dɔgɩsɩ Kapɛɛnam tɩmma a te sodɔm tɩmma.” Yesu basɩ tɩya nala dɩ ba ko ʋ lee a na wiyese Luka
translated: And when they had said this, the scripture which says that Jesus would suffer and rise on the third day was fulfilled." Jesus said this so that they would understand what was written about him in Luke

ref: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who truly knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
text: Ŋ Kuwo kpaa kɩŋ buloŋ dee tɩya ŋ nɛ. Ŋ Kuwo dʋŋ nɛ gyɩŋ gɛɛ ŋ aa kɩya. Ŋ dʋŋ mɛ rɛ gyɩŋ gɛɛ ŋ Kuwo aa kɩya. Nal buloŋ bɩ gyɩma ʋ, see nala hʋ dʋŋ ŋ aa lɩya a daga ʋ tɩya ba.”
translated: God did not create the world to leave me alone. God wants me to know him. I also want to know God. If the world is good for you, then you also want to give thanks to him.

ref: Some Pharisees noticed this and said to Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do this!”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Farasiima badɔmɔŋ na ba a pɩyɛsɩ Yesu a baa, “Ɩ bɩ naa? Kyɛwiyesii hʋ kyɛɛ sɩna daa ɩ hatɩnna hʋ kɩ kyogi gɛɛ.”
translated: Then the Pharisees also asked him how he had received his sight. He said to them, "He put mud on my eyes, and I washed, and I see."

ref: Jesus left and went into one of their synagogues,
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu lɩɩ doŋ, a mʋ gyʋʋ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwaldɩya kɩdɩgɩ,
translated: When Jesus had said this, he crossed the Kidron Valley,

ref: Some people brought to Jesus a man who was blind and could not talk because he had a demon in him. Jesus healed the man, and then he was able to talk and see.
text: Ɛɛ hal tɩyaŋ nɛ nala badɔmɔŋ kaŋ baal kɩdɩgɩ gyɩŋbɔmɔ aa kana, ʋ nyʋlɩmɩ, aŋ bɩ wuwo kɩ basɩ wɩya mɛ, a ko Yesu lee. Nyɛ rɛ Yesu tɩɩbɩ baal hʋ, ʋ bɩl wuwo kɩ basɩ wɩya aŋ kɩ na mɛ.
translated: And when the man with the unclean spirit who was living in the tombs saw him, he ran and fell down before him, and cried out with a loud voice saying, What have I to do with you, Jesus? When Jesus came to that place, he commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside because they wanted to talk with him.
text: Yesu ha gyɩ aa basɩ wɩya kɩ tɩya nɩgyamaa hʋ gɛɛ, dɩ ʋ naa abee ʋ naabalɩya ko sɩŋ gyaŋhal tɩyaŋ a kɩ kyɛ dɩ ba na ʋ.
translated: Jesus did not want people to know where he was, because he was teaching his disciples something that they should understand.

ref: Jesus used stories when he spoke to the people. In fact, he did not tell them anything without using stories.
text: Namagɩsɩ rɛ Yesu gyɩ aa kpa a basɩ wɩya no buloŋ kɩ tɩya gyamaa hʋ. Ʋ gyɩ bɩ sɩ basɩ wɩɩ buloŋ dɩ ʋ bɩ kaŋ namaga maga.
translated: The name Jesus means that he will save his people from their sins. He will save people from sin so that they can know God well.

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a shop owner is looking for fine pearls.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl baa, “Wɩɩsɩbee koro hʋ kɩɩ gɛɛ rɛ anɩɩ bayalʋʋ aa gɔllɩ kɩ kyɛ sɩfɩyaŋbʋyɩ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ pɛɛl.
translated: Then Jesus said to them, "Truly I tell you, some who are standing here will not taste death before they see the kingdom of God come with power."

ref: When the net is full, it is dragged to the shore, and the fishermen sit down to separate the fish. They keep the good ones, but throw the bad ones away.
text: Fuwonamɩɩsɩ hʋ aa ko su awule hʋ, fuwonaŋkasɩla hʋ kaŋ awule hʋ tarɩ lɩɩ nɩɩ hʋ nyʋwa, a tʋwasɩ fuwonaŋweliye hʋ buloŋ aŋ kpa kɩna hʋ aa bɩ yaa a yuwo ta.
translated: When you harvest your field, do not harvest to the very edges of your field or gather the gleanings of your harvest, but leave them for the poor and the foreigner.

ref: “Come on!” Jesus said. Peter then got out of the boat and started walking on the water toward him.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu baa, “Sii kɩ ko.” Ʋ aa basɩ gɛɛ, Piita sii lɩɩ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ tɩyaŋ a vala nɩɩ hʋ nyuu tɩyaŋ a kɩ mʋ Yesu lee.
translated: Then Jesus said, "Come." He got out of the boat, Peter walked on the water and went on the water to go to Jesus.

ref: John's followers took his body and buried it. Then they told Jesus what had happened. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Mark
text: Gyɔɔŋ hatɩnna hʋ gyɩ aa ko nɩɩ wɩɩ no, ba sii ko laa Gyɔɔŋ a kaŋ mʋ hogo, aŋ mʋ basɩ wɩɩ hʋ aa yaa a tɩya Yesu. Yesu tɩya nala tusi bɔnɔŋ kɩdiiliye Maakɩ
translated: When God's time comes that he will do this thing, he will send his son God, and his mother will be the one who will give birth to Jesus. Jesus' birth is written in the gospel of Mark

ref: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu yɩrɩ ʋ hatɩnna hʋ a basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ŋ aa naa nala no, sikii kana ŋ nɛ. Ba kyɛyɛ boto rɛ nyɛ ba aa we ŋ lee aŋ bɩ naa kɩdiiliye di. Ŋ bee kyɛ dɩ ŋ ta ba abee losi. Dɩ mɩyaŋ nɛ yaa gɛɛ, ba sɩ ko mʋ yiyegi ŋmanɩɩ tɩyaŋ.”
translated: Then Jesus turned to his disciples and said to them, "I tell you the truth, it is hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven. Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God."

ref: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
text: Ka ʋ kpa paanʋʋ gama hʋ bapɛ abee fuwonamɩɩsɩ hʋ a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ aŋgyʋwasʋ, aŋ kaŋ lɔgɔ lɔgɔ, a tɩya ʋ hatɩnna hʋ ba laa kpaa nɩgyamaa hʋ buloŋ.
translated: And if someone does not provide for his relatives, especially for his own household, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.

ref: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu sii mʋ Sizaariya Filipi paalʋʋ. Doŋ nɛ ʋ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ hatɩnna hʋ a baa, “Kɩbee rɛ nala baa dɩ mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye yaa?”
translated: When Jesus came to the region of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, "Who do people say the Son of Man is?"

ref: Simon Peter spoke up, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Siimɔŋ Piita baa, “Ɩ rɛ yaa Krisita hʋ, Wɩɩsɩ hʋ aa weye biye.”
translated: He said to Simon Peter, "You are the Christ, the Son of the living God."

ref: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Piita kana ʋ fasɩ lɩɩ saŋkala, a basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa “Ta basɩ gɛɛ! Ŋ Tɩɩna Wɩɩsɩ ta laa nyʋwa dɩ wɩɩ no maakyiye yaa ɩ.”
translated: When Peter wanted to defend himself with a sword, they told him "Don't defend yourself! My Father's will must be done in this world."

ref: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will find it.
text: Nal hʋ buloŋ aa kyɛ dɩ ʋ laa ʋ tɩɩ mɩɩbol, ʋ tɩɩna bɩ sɩ na ʋ mɩɩbol. Amɛ nal hʋ mɛ aa kuu ŋ wɩya a vɩya ʋ mɩɩbol ta ʋ tɩɩna sɩ na mɩɩbol aa bɩ kaŋ tenii.
translated: If a person refuses to accept his mistake, he will not learn from his mistake. But if a person admits and accepts his mistake, then he will learn from the mistake and it will not happen again.

ref: There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. His face was shining like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
text: Ba ko baa dɩ ba deŋ, dɩ ba naa Yesu bɩrɩma, ʋ sɩya buloŋ kɩ tʋla gɛɛ anɩɩ wɩɩpɔsɩɩ aŋka ʋ gannɩ mɛ pʋllɩ gɛɛ peretete a kɩ tʋla.
translated: And when they came and saw Jesus' power, they were very afraid because he had healed the man who was completely mad.

ref: When the disciples heard the voice, they were so afraid they fell flat on the ground.
text: Yesu hatɩnna hʋ aa nɩɩ taal hʋ gɛɛ, kambɩŋ kaŋ ba, ba tuu tele kyige taŋha.
translated: When Jesus had finished saying all this, he left with his disciples and went across the Kidron Valley.

ref: I brought him to your disciples, but none of them could heal him.”
text: Ŋ kana ʋ ko ɩ hatɩnna hʋ lee rɛ, amɛ ba bɩ wuwo tɩɩbɩ ʋ.”
translated: I tell you that you have done it well, but they are not pleased with you.

ref: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu nɔsɩ gyɩŋbɔŋ hʋ ʋ lɩɩ biye hʋ tɩyaŋ, ka biye hʋ teŋ pirigi deye.
translated: When Jesus entered the town where the woman lived, the whole town came out.

ref: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
text: Ba bɩl bɩ yaa nala balɩya, ba bɩrɩma nɩdɩgɩ rɛ. Ɛɛwɩya, nala hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa leŋ ba yaa nɩdɩgɩ ʋ bɩ maga dɩ nihuwobiŋ kaŋ ba porigi dɔmɔŋ.”
translated: When they wanted to kill them, they changed their minds. However, the Lord had shown them that it was not good to shed the blood of their brothers.

ref: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
text: Ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma rɛ, a lɩɩ sɔŋsɔŋ hal, dɩ baal lɛ kuu wɩɩ buloŋ wɩya a vɩya ʋ haaŋ, aŋ bɩl mʋ kpa hadɩma, ɩ tɩɩna yaa basɔŋsɔnnɩ rɛ.”
translated: If you do not believe me, he will surely come, and when he comes he will find you in your house, and he will kill you completely, it will be finished with you forever.

ref: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
text: Baala hʋ aa laa hal mʋ baga hʋ, ʋ tuŋ ba kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ kyɛdɩgɩ tuno.
translated: When a child refuses to listen to their mother, they will face difficulties in the future.

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: Ŋ bɩ kaŋ ŋmanɩɩ dɩ ŋ kaŋ ŋ molbiye yaa gɛɛ hʋ ŋ aa kyɛɛ? Koo ma tɩsɩ aa hɔllɩ akuu ŋ aa kaŋ bɔnyɛ wɩya rɛ?”
translated: If I don't know something and I tell you I don't know, will you be angry with me? Or is it better that I lie to you and tell you something false?

ref: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
text: Zebedi haaŋ gyɩ sii kaŋ ʋ biisi balɩya ko Yesu lee rɛ a tuu gbinni ʋ sɩya tɩyaŋ, aŋ sʋla ʋ dɩ ʋ pɛ ʋ bee wɩɩ kɩdɩgɩ.
translated: Zebedee's sons came to Jesus and asked him to do whatever they wanted, and he asked them what they wanted him to do for them.

ref: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Farasiima sii lɩɩ a vʋʋlɩ gɛɛ hʋ ba aa sɩ magɩsɩ Yesu a bara kana ʋ abee wɩpɩyɛsɩya.
translated: And the Pharisees went out and held a council against Jesus how they might destroy him.

ref: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
text: Nal hʋ buloŋ aa kpa ʋ tɩɩ yohuŋ, Wɩɩsɩ sɩ kana ʋ hʋwɔlɩ, ka nal hʋ mɛ aa kaŋ ʋ tɩɩ hʋwɔlɩ, Wɩɩsɩ sɩ kpa ʋ yohuŋ.
translated: If a person loves his body (physical self), God will hate his soul, but if a person hates his body (physical self), God will love his soul.

ref: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
text: Ba aa yaa wɩɩ buloŋ a kɩ kyɛ dɩ nala na ba rɛ. Ba aa sabɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩya a we dagibiisi tɩyaŋ nɛ a kpa vʋwa ba gannɩ nosi tɩyaŋ abee ba nyupugini tɩyaŋ, aŋ leŋ ba gannɩ nyʋʋgyʋwasa mɛ doliye doliye a te ɛɛ hʋ nɩwaasɩma kɩna aa kɩya.
translated: They want to continue to do what is right. They know God's word and his wonderful works and they remember his good deeds and his promises, and they also remember the blessings he has given them in the past which he has done.

ref: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
text: Mamaa nyʋlʋŋ-sɩlaala, ma sɩ na tʋwara! Ma aa baa, “Dɩ nal lɛ ŋmɩyɛsɩ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ, ʋ bɩ yaa ŋmɩyɛsʋʋ amɛ dɩ ʋ rɛ ko ŋmɩyɛsɩ salma kɩna hʋ aa we Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ, ʋ yaa ŋmɩyɛsʋʋ rɛ.”
translated: My brothers and sisters, do not grumble! You have said, "If a person serves God, he does good things so that when the time of judgment comes, God will do good to him."

ref: That's what you are like. Outside you look good, but inside you are evil and only pretend to be good.
text: Ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ ma mɛ kɩɩ nala sɩya tɩyaŋ. Dɩ ba aa rɛ na ma, ba aa bɩɩnɩ nɩɩ ma yaa nɩtɩɩma rɛ, ka ma tɩsɩ su abee wɩbɔmɔ abee minaafigi wɩya.
translated: If it happens that I don't get a job this year. If they don't call me, they don't give me work or something like that, then I will go back to school or learn a trade.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: Saŋa no tɩyaŋ dɩ nal buloŋ nɛ ko basɩ tɩya ɩ a baa, ‘Na Krisita hʋ we daha, koo ʋ we lee kɩdɩgɩ, ta laa ʋ tɩɩna di.’
translated: If someone comes to you and does not bring this teaching, do not receive him into your house, and do not greet him.

ref: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
text: Ɛɛwɩya ma yaŋ gyɩŋ tɩya ma tɩɩ weliŋ, beewɩya ma bɩ gyɩŋ kyɛɛ hʋ ma Tɩɩna aa sɩ mɩɩgɩ ko.
translated: If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves and the Truth is not in us.

text: Ma mɛ yaŋ yaa siri pɔ, beewɩya mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye sɩ mɩɩgɩ ko saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ ma aa bɩ yɩyɛla.” Tʋŋtʋnnɩ buloŋ maga ʋ kaŋ wɩtɩɩ rɛ Luka

translated: A person who does not have a good heart or whose heart is not right, even if you give them advice, they will not accept it, but a humble person will accept advice and benefit from it.

translated: When a person hears the word but does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what was sown in his heart. When he hears the Word of God, but does not accept it, Satan comes and takes away what was sown in him so that he will not believe.

text: Yesu bɩl basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Wɩɩsɩ koro hʋ kɩɩ gɛɛ rɛ anɩɩ nal aa siye a mʋ gyaasɩ kɩdoho ʋ baga tɩyaŋ.

translated: When someone is sick with leprosy, he should not live among people in the town and he must not go, he must stay outside alone until he recovers.

translated: If a child does not listen to advice, then that child will learn through suffering. If a child refuses to listen, they will learn the hard way.

translated: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, "Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?" When Jesus heard this, he said to them, "It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick." Matthew

translated: If you want to build a house, you must build it on the ground, not in the air. You must lay its foundation well.

text: Ba aa ko ba naa dɩ Yesu hatɩnna hʋ badɔmɔŋ bɩ sɔŋ nɩɩ aŋ kɩ di kɩna. Nyɛ yaa bisiŋ nɛ ba lesiri tɩyaŋ.
translated: Those who did not believe in Jesus were angry and wanted to kill him. But they were afraid of the crowd.

ref: The Pharisees came out and started an argument with Jesus. They wanted to test him by asking for a sign from heaven.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Farasiima badɔmɔŋ sii ko Yesu lee, a kɩ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ wɩya. Ba gyɩ aa kyɛ dɩ ba magɩsɩ ʋ na wɩya, ba baa dɩ ʋ yaa wɩmagɩl dɩ ba na a gyɩma anɩɩ ʋ dee hʋ lɩɩ Wɩɩsɩ lee rɛ.
translated: When the Pharisees heard what Jesus said, they were not happy. They thought that they would trap him in his words, so they asked him whether it was right to pay taxes to Caesar or not.

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?” “You are the Messiah!” Peter replied.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl pɩyɛsɩ ʋ hatɩnna hʋ mɛ a baa, “Ka ma mɛ, ma baa ŋ yaa aŋnɛ rɛ?” Piita baa “Ɩ rɛ yaa Krisita hʋ, laataal hʋ Wɩɩsɩ a lɩya.”
translated: And when Jesus asked his disciples what they said, "Who do you say I am?" Peter said "You are the Christ, the Son of the living God."

ref: After Jesus and the disciples had gone back home and were alone, they asked him, “Why couldn't we force out that demon?”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bee ʋ hatɩnna hʋ mʋ gyʋʋ dɩya. Ba lʋwɔlɩ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Bee rɛ tɩŋ á kɛ bɩ wuwo kil gyɩŋbɔŋ hʋ?”
translated: Then Jesus asked his disciples this question. They answered him saying, "Who do people say that I am?"

ref: At once the boy's father shouted, “I do have faith! Please help me to have even more.”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ biye hʋ kuwo sii sɩŋ a heeli basɩ a baa, “Ŋ laa ɩ di rɛ, amɛ ʋ bɩ maga. Pɛ ŋ tɩyaŋ dɩ ŋ laa ɩ di yʋga.”
translated: And when the child refused to eat, he told his father, "I will eat it, but you are a fool. Because I worked so I will eat it all."

ref: Jesus left with his disciples and started through Galilee. He did not want anyone to know about it,
text: Ɛɛ rɛ ba sii lɩɩ doŋ, a tuu tɩŋ Galili paalʋʋ mʋ. Yesu fa bee kyɛ dɩ nal buloŋ gyɩŋ lee hʋ ʋ aa we.
translated: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret. And when the men of that place recognized Jesus, they sent word to all that region.

ref: If your foot causes you to sin, chop it off. You would be better off to go into life lame than to have two feet and be thrown into hell.
text: Dɩ ɩ naaŋ nɛ aa kyɛ dɩ ʋ kana ɩ we wɩbɔmɔ yayɩ tɩyaŋ, keri ta. Ɩ aa sɩ mʋ wɩɩsɩbee abee naakuu, ʋ rɛ kpɩya ba aa sɩ kpa ɩ yuwo we diŋ tɩyaŋ abee naapilime. ‘Lee hʋ kyaaŋkyʋwana aa sɩ di ɩ teŋbii deŋdeŋ a bɩ sɩ sʋba, ka diŋ hʋ mɛ bɩ sɩ dɩsɩ.’
translated: If he refuses to listen to them, tell it to the church. If he refuses to listen even to the church, let him be to you as a Gentile and a tax collector. 'For where two or three are gathered in my name, there am I among them.'

ref: After Jesus sat down and told the twelve disciples to gather around him, he said, “If you want the place of honor, you must become a slave and serve others!”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu hɔŋ aŋ yɩrɩ ʋ hatɩnna fi abee balɩya hʋ, a basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Nal hʋ aa kyɛ dɩ ʋ yaa ma tɩyaŋ nɩbal, see dɩ ʋ kaŋ ʋ tɩɩ bɩrɩmɩ ma tɩyaŋ haŋbiye a tʋma tɩya ma buloŋ.”
translated: When Jesus saw that his teaching pleased them, he continued to tell them, "The kingdom of God is not like what you think, but it is like a farmer who scattered seed in his field."

ref: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
text: Azɔ mɛ sii lʋl Zadɔkɩ. Zadɔkɩ mɛ sii lʋl Akimu. Akimu mɛ sii lʋl Ɛliyudu.
translated: Then Zadok had a son. Zadok had a son Achim. Achim had a son Eliud.

ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: Ʋ gyɩ aa bɩɩnɩ gɛɛ tɩyaŋ nɛ, malɩka ko ʋ lee duwoso tɩyaŋ a basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Gyosɛfʋ, Kuwori Deviti doho nal, ta leŋ dɩ kambɩŋ kana ɩ, ɩ aa sɩ kpa Mɛɛrɩ a yaa ɩ haaŋ, beewɩya Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ dee rɛ tɩŋ ʋ kaŋ biye no luwol.
translated: While he was thinking about this, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, "Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because what is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.

ref: Herod brought together the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses and asked them, “Where will the Messiah be born?”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ gyɩ yɩrɩ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ abee Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla hʋ a ko laŋŋɩ, a pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Lee tɩyaŋ nɛ ba sɩ lʋl Krisita hʋ, laataal hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa lɩya?”
translated: And when he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to arrest Peter also during the days of Unleavened Bread, and he put him in prison, saying, "After the Passover when they finish celebrating it, I will bring him out before the people?"

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
text: Ma kɩ yaa wɩya hʋ aa daga anɩɩ ma bɩrɩma lɩɩ ma wɩbɔmɔ tɩyaŋ nɛ,
translated: If I go there and he gives me my money or my things,

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Nyɔgɩmaa pʋna gannɩ rɛ Gyɔɔŋ gyɩ fa kɩ laalɩ, aŋ aa kpa tɛpɩɩŋbii vʋwa ʋ tɩŋa. Nyɩŋgɩrɩgɩsɩ abee tʋʋ rɛ gyɩ yaa ʋ kɩdiiliye.
translated: The woman went to the market and bought food, and she cooked it for her husband. In the evening they ate it together.

ref: Many Pharisees and Sadducees also came to be baptized. But John said to them: You bunch of snakes! Who warned you to run from the coming judgment?
text: Amɛ Gyɔɔŋ aa ko na dɩ Farasiima abee Sadusiima yʋga aa kɩ ko ʋ lee dɩ ʋ fo ba wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ gɛɛ, ɛɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ɛɛdɔŋ dʋma! Ma dawarɩ yʋga ma tɩyaŋ! Ma bɩɩna ma sɩ wuwo fá nyʋgɩsɩ kyɛɛ hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa sɩ di nala sarɩya rɛ koo?
translated: When Jesus saw that the Pharisees and Sadducees had come and wanted him to show them a sign, he answered and said to them, "You hypocrites! You know how to interpret the weather! How is it that you cannot understand the signs of the times that God is showing?"

ref: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
text: Ʋ kol ʋ mɩkyaarɩgbaha rɛ, dɩ ʋ ko ŋmaa ʋ mɩya buloŋ teŋ, dɩ ʋ kpa gbaha hʋ a kyaarɩ lɩɩ miŋbii hʋ a we ʋ busol tɩyaŋ aŋ kpa mɩŋgyɔrʋ hʋ we diŋ tɩyaŋ. Diŋ hʋ aa bɩ sɩ maakyiye dɩsɩ.” Gyɔɔŋ foo Yesu wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ Maakɩ
translated: When you see the abomination of desolation standing where it should not be (let the reader understand), then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains, and let the one who is on the housetop not come down or enter to take anything out of his house, and let the one who is in the field not turn back to take his cloak. But woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing infants in those days." From the Gospel of Jesus according to Mark

ref: “Listen, lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, lands along the road to the sea and across the Jordan. Listen Galilee, land of the Gentiles!
text: Ʋ gyɩ baa, “Ma nala hʋ aa we Zebulɔŋ abee Nafitali paalʋʋ aa kyaasɩ Galili fuwobal hʋ, Gyɔɔdaŋ fuwobal hʋ kyolo, abee Galili paalʋʋ nala, lee hʋ tɩyaŋ nala hʋ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma aa yʋga, ma nɩɩ daha,
translated: He said, "The people living in the land of Zebulun and Naphtali, toward the sea of Galilee, beyond the Jordan, and in Galilee of the Gentiles, the people who sat in darkness have seen a great light,"

ref: While Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw two brothers. One was Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting their net into the lake.
text: Yesu gyɩ kpaa Galili fuwobal hʋ nyʋwa a kpala kɩ baŋ dɩ ʋ naa fuwonaŋkasɩla balɩya dɩ ba aa kasɩ fuwonamɩɩsɩ abee ba keliŋsi. Ba gyɩ yaa nɩmmabalɩya rɛ. Kɩdɩgɩ feŋ nɛ yaa Siimɔŋ Ʋ naabiye hʋ mɛ feŋ nɛ Aŋduru.
translated: Jesus was walking by the Sea of Galilee when he saw two brothers who were fishermen casting their nets into the sea. They were fishermen. One was called Simon, his brother, and the other was Andrew.

ref: At once they left the boat and their father and went with Jesus. Jesus Teaches, Preaches, and Heals (Luke
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu yɩrɩ ba, ba guu sii a leŋ ba nɩɩduworiboro hʋ abee ba kuwo buloŋ ta, aŋ tɩŋ Yesu hal. Yesu tɩɩbɩ wɩɩla tɩmma Luka
translated: When Jesus saw them, they were standing at a distance because they were lepers, and they called out to Jesus. The Gospel according to Luke

ref: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
text: Nyusʋŋtɩmma rɛ yaa nala hʋ aa kaŋ tɩpʋlʋŋ Wɩɩsɩ sɩya tɩyaŋ ba aa rɛ sɩ na Wɩɩsɩ.
translated: The Jews did not believe that he had been blind and received his sight until they called the parents of the man who had received his sight.

ref: leave your gift there in front of the altar. Make peace with that person, then come back and offer your gift to God.
text: ʋ maga dɩ ɩ leŋ wɩɩsʋllɩ kɩna hʋ kogu hʋ sɩya tɩyaŋ nɛ aŋ mɩɩgɩ mʋ, dɩ ɩ bee ɩ dɔŋtɩɩna hʋ marɩ dɔmɔŋ, ka dɩ ɩ na mɩɩgɩ mʋ kpa kɩna hʋ a sʋla Wɩɩsɩ.
translated: If you do not listen to the word of God and do not fear his great and awesome name, he will send terrible plagues on you and your descendants, severe and lasting plagues, and you will not escape from them unless you return to God.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: Ma bɩl nɩya dɩ Wɩɩsɩ gyɩ basɩ tɩya faafaa nala rɛ a baa, “Dɩ ɩ rɛ ŋmɩyɛsɩ Wɩɩsɩ anɩɩ ɩ sɩ yaa wɩɩ, ta ko vɩya wɩɩ hʋ yayɩ. Amɛ yaa wɩɩ hʋ ɩ aa ŋmɩyɛsa baa dɩ ɩ sɩ yaa hʋ.”
translated: And I also heard the voice of the Lord saying to the people, "If you truly love the Lord and you want to do his will, then do not fear anything. But doing his will, you must love it so that you can do it."

ref: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
text: Dɩ ɩ rɛ kaŋ kɩŋ, dɩ nal sʋla, kpa tɩya ʋ. Dɩ nal lɛ ko ɩ lee a kɩ kyɛ kɩŋ dɩ ʋ kyɩma, dɩ ɩ rɛ kana, kpa kyɩma ʋ. Yesu baa á kyo á dɔmɔ Luka
translated: If he comes at midnight, or even near dawn, and finds them ready, those servants will be blessed. The Gospel of Jesus Christ according to Luke

ref: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
text: Amɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma rɛ, ma kyo ma dɔmɔ aŋ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ kɩ tɩya nala hʋ mɛ aa dɔgɩsɩ ma.
translated: If we do not do it, we will see that God will do it and show us.

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: Dɩ ma ko kɩ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ, ma ta kɩ basɩ wɩgyamaa aa bɩ kaŋ memii, anɩɩ nala hʋ aa bɩ gyɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ aa kɩ yaa gɛɛ. Ba bɩɩna anɩɩ ba wɩbasɩgyamaa no rɛ sɩ leŋ dɩ Wɩɩsɩ nɩɩ ba kyʋwala.
translated: If we say that we have fellowship with God, and we walk in darkness, or we do things that God does not like. We are lying and we are not doing the truth as God wants us to fellowship with him.

ref: Don't be like them. Your Father knows what you need even before you ask.
text: Ma ta kɩɩ ba. Beewɩya ma Kuwo kɛ gyɩŋ kɩŋ hʋ ma aa kyɛ rɛ, dɩ ma ha gba bɩ sʋla ʋ.
translated: I don't know them. But God knows the thing that I will do, when I come out I will repay them.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: Ɛɛwɩya, ma ta leŋ dɩ kyiye wɩya kyɩl ma. Kyɛɛ buloŋ kaŋ ʋ wɩgyʋʋlɩya rɛ. Ma ta bɩl kpaa wɩdʋma buloŋ pɛ kyɛɛ kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ bee ʋ wɩgyʋʋlɩya tɩyaŋ.”
translated: Yes, I will do what you say. Because the matter is very serious. I will not tell anyone about the matter or let anyone know about it.

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: Ba bɩ sɩ wuwo kpa dʋŋdɔɔdɩgɩ a dɔbɔ dʋŋdɔɔ-nɩmma balɩya, beewɩya ʋ sɩ kyo kɩdɩgɩ aŋ haasɩ kɩdɩgɩ mɛ. Ʋ sɩ kɩ tɩŋ kɩdɩgɩ nyʋwa weliŋ aŋ kɩ vɩya kɩdɩgɩ hʋ mɛ nyʋwa. Ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ nal buloŋ mɛ bɩ sɩ wuwo kyo dʋnɩya no kɩna aŋ bɩl tɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ mɛ.
translated: If they do not accept the message of the gospel, do not force them to believe or accept it. If they do not want to listen and do not want to receive it, leave them alone. But today in this world some people force others to believe and follow God.

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: Ɛɛwɩya ma ta leŋ kɩdiiliye hʋ ma aa sɩ di, koo kɩŋ hʋ ma aa sɩ nyʋwa, koo kɩŋ hʋ mɛ ma aa sɩ laalɩ wɩya kyɩl ma.
translated: If you do not know the answer to a question, do not say you know it, or say you heard it, or make up something to say.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Ma deŋ na digibiisi, ba bee pala, ba bɩ kaŋ busolo mɛ a kɩ we kɩdiiliye, amɛ á Kuwo Wɩɩsɩ aa we wɩɩsɩbee tɩyaŋ nɛ aa tɩya ba kɩdiiliye. Ma yaŋ daa kaŋ tɔnɔ a te digibiisi no?
translated: When we sin, they don't forgive us, they keep it in their hearts and don't forget it, but God our Father forgives our sins and forgets our wrongdoings. So why should we keep unforgiveness in our hearts?

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Nala hʋ aa bɩ gyɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ dʋnɩya no tɩyaŋ, ba aa rɛ aa leŋ kɩna no wɩya kyɩl ba. Ma Kuwo aa we wɩɩsɩbee tɩyaŋ gyɩma anɩɩ ma maga dɩ ma kaŋ kɩna no buloŋ nɛ.
translated: For God so loved the world that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believes in him should not perish but have everlasting life.

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
text: Ɛɛ rɛ ɩ sɩ yaa basɩ tɩya ɩ dɔŋtɩɩna a baa dɩ ʋ leŋ dɩ ɩ lɩɩ gyagɩŋ hʋ aa we ʋ sɩɩ tɩyaŋ ta, aŋ bɩ sɩ wuwo lɩɩ daakuu hʋ aa we ɩ tɩɩ sɩɩ tɩyaŋ kɛ.
translated: If you want to know whether your friend will give you the thing you asked him for or whether he will give you something else, then you should look at the day on which you asked him for it.

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Ɛɛdɔŋ minaafigi tɩɩna! Laa sɩya lɩɩ daakuu hʋ aa we ɩ tɩɩ sɩɩ tɩyaŋ dɩ ɩ sɩya wuwo kɩ na weliŋ, dɩ ɩ na wuwo lɩɩ gyagɩŋ hʋ aa we ɩ dɔŋtɩɩna sɩɩ tɩyaŋ.
translated: You foolish person! If someone gives you something and you don't know how to use it properly and you use it wrongly, then you will use it in a way that will harm you.

ref: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
text: Ba wɩyaalɩya tɩyaŋ nɛ ma sɩ tɩŋa a gyɩŋ ba. Beewɩya sɔgyaŋ bee nɔŋ kyʋkyɔyɩ, sugulugu mɛ bee nɔŋ tutogi-naŋkpɩlɩya.
translated: They were not able to defeat us because we were stronger than them. They had many weapons and also had vehicles, but we had determination and courage.

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Tɩya hʋ aa kaŋ laaŋfɩya bɩ sɩ wuwo nɔŋ nɔŋbɔŋlɔrɩnɩ, tɩya hʋ mɛ aa bɩ kaŋ laaŋfɩya bɩ sɩ wuwo nɔŋ nɔŋweliye.
translated: The day he went to the farm with his younger brother, that day he also went to the farm with his older brother.

ref: Anyone who hears my teachings and doesn't obey them is like a foolish person who built a house on sand.
text: Amɛ nal hʋ mɛ buloŋ aa gyegile nɩɩ wɩya no ŋ aa basɩ, aŋ bee tɩŋ ba, ʋ tɩɩna kɩɩ gɛɛ rɛ anɩɩ bambugi aa saa ʋ dɩya sɩgɩ bumbugulo tɩyaŋ.
translated: If someone has a wound on their leg and it is not healing well, they should go and find the bamboo tree and use its sap to treat the wound.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Ŋ sɩ ko leŋ dɩ ʋ deye.”
translated: "Yes, and Jesus said to him, 'I will come and heal him.'"

ref: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
text: Nyɛ rɛ ba buloŋ nyʋʋsɩ fɩyɛlɩ, ba kɩ pɩyɛsɩ dɔmɔŋ a baa, “Nɩgɛɛ iriŋ nɛ nyɛ? Fuwoni abee puwoni buloŋ kɩ tɩŋa ʋ nyʋwa?” Yesu kile gyɩŋsɩ ta baala balɩya tɩyaŋ Maakɩ
translated: When they saw the great miracle, they asked each other in amazement, "Who is this man? Even the wind and the waves obey him!" Jesus then crossed to the other side of Lake Galilee

ref: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu sii kpa mʋ Gadara paalʋʋ fuwo hʋ kyolo. Dɩ baala balɩya lɩɩ bilni tɩyaŋ a ko kyeme ʋ. Gyɩŋbɔmɔ rɛ gyɩ kaŋ baala no ba kɩ yaarɩ. Nal buloŋ gyɩ bɩ wuwo kɩ tɩŋ ŋmanɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ baala no balɩya aa we, beewɩya, ba gyɩ bɔmɔ rɛ weliŋ.
translated: When Jesus arrived on the other side in the region of the Gadarenes, two demon-possessed men coming from the tombs met him. They were so violent that no one could pass that way. And it was in the tombs that these demon-possessed men lived, naked, and no one could bind them anymore, even with chains.

ref: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
text: Nyɛ rɛ porikudaala hʋ fá mʋ gyʋʋ bee hʋ tɩyaŋ a basɩ wɩɩ hʋ buloŋ aa yaa doŋ abee wɩɩ hʋ aa yaa baala hʋ gyɩ aa kaŋ gyɩŋbɔmɔ hʋ mɛ tɩyaŋ a tɩya bee hʋ nala.
translated: If a politician wants to do good and is seeking the people's welfare or wants to help them or wants to do something that will benefit the community and is seeking to do good.

ref: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Yesu bɩl aa lɩɩ doŋ kɩ mʋ, nyʋlma balɩya mɛ sii kɩ tɩŋa ʋ hal aŋ huwori kɩ yɩrɩ ʋ a baa, “Yesu, Deviti Biye, fá á sikii.”
translated: Jesus was passing by when a blind man heard about it and shouted to him saying, "Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me."

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Matthew's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners were also there.
text: Nyɛ rɛ Yesu bee ʋ hatɩnna hʋ buloŋ kpa mʋ Matiwu dɩya, a hɔŋ kɩ di kɩna. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ lampolaala bee wɩbɔŋyaala mɛ ko pɛ ba tɩyaŋ, ba buloŋ kɩ di kɩna hʋ.
translated: When Jesus went to Matthew's house to eat, many tax collectors and sinners also came and ate with them.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, an official came and knelt in front of him. The man said, “My daughter has just now died! Please come and place your hand on her. Then she will live again.”

translated: When Jesus saw the crowds, he had compassion on them, because they were harassed and helpless. He said to his disciples, "The harvest is plentiful but the workers are few. Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into his harvest field."

translated: And the Pharisees and the teachers of the law saw him eating with sinners and tax collectors. Jesus answered them.

text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ma ta kɩ fáá nala hʋ aa sɩ wuwo kpʋ ma dʋŋ. Amɛ ma kɩ fá Wɩɩsɩ, beewɩya ʋ rɛ kaŋ dee ʋ aa sɩ wuwo leŋ dɩ ɩ sʋba, a kaŋ dee mɛ ʋ aa sɩ maakyiye kpa ɩ we diŋ.
translated: And Jesus said to them, "I did not come down so that the world would hate me. But I came to testify, because its deeds are evil and I testify that its deeds are not good and they are bad."

text: Nal dɩya nala tɩɩ rɛ sɩ yaa ʋ dɔmɔ, a haasɩ ʋ te nal buloŋ.
translated: If a child does not listen to what you say, you leave him to the child's foolishness.

ref: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
text: ba mʋ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Basɩ tɩya ma, ɩ rɛ yaa nal hʋ Gyɔɔŋ fa aa baa dɩ ʋ sɩ ko laa ma ta hʋ, koo á ha kɩ gyegili nɩdɩma?”

text: Dɩ ɛɛ yaŋ daa, nɩgɛɛ iriŋ nɛ ma lɩɩ dɩ ma na? Ma lɩɩ dɩ ma na nal a laalɩ gaweliye rɛ? Aayɩ. Deŋ na, nala hʋ aa laalɩ gannɩ no iriŋ, ba aa we kuworidɩɩsɩ tɩyaŋ nɛ.
translated: If it is like that, why should I go there with you? Should I go there with you to become a slave? No. So then, whoever becomes a slave there, they will treat them like a dog.

translated: I want to ask you something that I don't understand. Do you know what God's kingdom is? If you answer me, something I also don't know, it is God's kingdom.

text: ‘Á yɩɩ yɩɩla rɛ tɩya ma, ka ma vɩya gʋwayɩ. Á taa haŋyee mɛ tɩya ma ka ma vɩya wiyi.”’
translated: "If you do good to me, I will do good to you. You should not repay me with evil."

ref: So I tell you on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than you. Come to Me and Rest (Luke
text: Amɛ ŋ yaŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma rɛ, sarɩya hʋ kyɛdiilii Wɩɩsɩ sɩ dɔgɩsɩ Kapɛɛnam tɩmma a te sodɔm tɩmma.” Yesu basɩ tɩya nala dɩ ba ko ʋ lee a na wiyese Luka
translated: And when they saw it, they all grumbled, saying, "He has gone in to be the guest of a man who is a sinner." This is what Jesus said and they said about him, as written in Luke

text: Ŋ Kuwo kpaa kɩŋ buloŋ dee tɩya ŋ nɛ. Ŋ Kuwo dʋŋ nɛ gyɩŋ gɛɛ ŋ aa kɩya. Ŋ dʋŋ mɛ rɛ gyɩŋ gɛɛ ŋ Kuwo aa kɩya. Nal buloŋ bɩ gyɩma ʋ, see nala hʋ dʋŋ ŋ aa lɩya a daga ʋ tɩya ba.”
translated: God does not want you to die. God wants you to be like him knowing good and evil. You also want to be like God knowing good and evil. If you eat the fruit, then you will know what is good and what is bad.

translated: He did this so that what the Lord said through his prophet Isaiah would come true. He said, "He himself took our sicknesses and carried our diseases." Jesus healed many people

text: Gyɔɔŋ hatɩnna hʋ gyɩ aa ko nɩɩ wɩɩ no, ba sii ko laa Gyɔɔŋ a kaŋ mʋ hogo, aŋ mʋ basɩ wɩɩ hʋ aa yaa a tɩya Yesu. Yesu tɩya nala tusi bɔnɔŋ kɩdiiliye Maakɩ
translated: When God's time came that he wanted to send his word, he sent his son who is called God's lamb, and his mother's name who gave birth to Jesus. Jesus' birth is written in the book called Mark

translated: Then Jesus turned to his disciples and said to them, "I tell you the truth, it is hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven. Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God."

translated: When Peter wanted to deny him the third time, they said to him "Don't deny! Your speech reveals that you are one of his disciples."

translated: If a person does not want to give you his thing, he does not have to give you his thing. But if a person has promised to give you his thing, he must give you the thing as he promised.

text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu baa, “Ma gyɩnaŋ nala aa bɩ laa Wɩɩsɩ di, ma yaa nɩbɔmɔ rɛ. Abaa! Kyɛyɛ baŋmɛ rɛ ŋ tɩɩ baa ŋ we ma lee?” Doŋ nɛ ka ʋ basɩ tɩya baal hʋ a baa, “Kaŋ biye hʋ ko daha.”

translated: When Jesus saw that a crowd was running together to him, he said, "What are you arguing about with them?"

ref: “Yes, he does,” Peter answered. After they had returned home, Jesus went up to Peter and asked him, “Simon, what do you think? Do the kings of this earth collect taxes and fees from their own people or from foreigners?”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Piita baa dɩ ʋ aa tuŋ nɛ. Piita aa basɩ gɛɛ aŋ mɩɩgɩ gyʋʋ dɩya, Yesu laa sɩya pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Piita, ɛɛ rɛ ɩ bɩɩnɩ? Kɩbee rɛ aa tuŋ lampoo a kɩ tɩya dʋnɩya no kuworo, tɔɔ hʋ nala tɩɩ koo nɩhʋwala?”
translated: Then Peter came to him with a question. Peter had been thinking about many things, so Jesus said to him, "Peter, what is troubling you? How many times should a person forgive their brother who sins against them, up to seven times?"

ref: Don't be cruel to any of these little ones! I promise you their angels are always with my Father in heaven.
text: “Mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye ko dɩ ŋ kyɛ a laa nala hʋ aa nyʋgɩsa rɛ a ta.” Yesu maga piyesi hʋ aa nyʋgɩsa namaga Luka
translated: Jesus told this parable to some who trusted in themselves that they were righteous.

ref: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
text: Ŋ marɩ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma rɛ, dɩ ma balɩya rɛ yaa nyʋʋdɩgɩ a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ kɩ kyɛ kɩŋ, ŋ Kuwo aa we wɩɩsɩbee tɩyaŋ sɩ yaa ʋ tɩya ma.
translated: I want to tell you my story, about my life and how I came to know God and what He has done in my life.

ref: Peter came up to the Lord and asked, “How many times should I forgive someone who does something wrong to me? Is seven times enough?”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Piita ko Yesu lee a pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Tɩɩna, dɩ ŋ dɔŋtɩɩna rɛ yaa wɩɩ kyogi ŋ, naaŋsɩɩnyʋwa bʋŋmɛ rɛ ŋ maga dɩ ŋ kpa kyɛ ʋ? Ŋ kpa kyɛ ʋ naaŋsɩɩnyʋwa bʋpɛ koo?”
translated: Then Peter came to Jesus and asked him, "Lord, how many times should I forgive my brother when he sins against me? Should I forgive him seven times?"

ref: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
text: Ba bɩl bɩ yaa nala balɩya, ba bɩrɩma nɩdɩgɩ rɛ. Ɛɛwɩya, nala hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa leŋ ba yaa nɩdɩgɩ ʋ bɩ maga dɩ nihuwobiŋ kaŋ ba porigi dɔmɔŋ.”
translated: When they saw that the child was dead, they began to cry. However, the child that God had given them as a gift was in their hearts and they would never forget.

ref: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
text: Ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma rɛ, a lɩɩ sɔŋsɔŋ hal, dɩ baal lɛ kuu wɩɩ buloŋ wɩya a vɩya ʋ haaŋ, aŋ bɩl mʋ kpa hadɩma, ɩ tɩɩna yaa basɔŋsɔnnɩ rɛ.”
translated: If you do not listen to me, he will be very angry, and the people will kill you with your entire household, and nothing will remain, it will be completely destroyed.

ref: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Bee rɛ tɩŋ ɩ kɩ pɩyɛsɩ ŋ wɩweliye wɩya? Wɩɩsɩ dʋŋ nɛ weliye. Dɩ ɩ rɛ aa kyɛ dɩ ɩ kaŋ mɩɩbol hʋ aa bɩ kaŋ tenii, kɩ tɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ mɩrɩsɩ hʋ ba aa saba biŋ.”
translated: Then Jesus asked him, "Why do you call me good? No one is good except God alone. You know the commandments: Do not murder, do not commit adultery, do not steal, do not give false testimony, honor your father and mother."

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ hatɩnna hʋ a baa, “Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma, ʋ yaa wɩduwo rɛ abee kɩna tɩɩna aa sɩ wuwo gyʋʋ wɩɩsɩbee koro hʋ.
translated: And Jesus said to his disciples, "Truly I say to you, it is difficult for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.

ref: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
text: Baala hʋ aa laa hal mʋ baga hʋ, ʋ tuŋ ba kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ kyɛdɩgɩ tuno.
translated: When a child refuses to listen to their mother, they will face difficulties in the future.

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: Ŋ bɩ kaŋ ŋmanɩɩ dɩ ŋ kaŋ ŋ molbiye yaa gɛɛ hʋ ŋ aa kyɛɛ? Koo ma tɩsɩ aa hɔllɩ akuu ŋ aa kaŋ bɔnyɛ wɩya rɛ?”
translated: If I don't know you and I don't know your family, how can I trust you? Or do you think I am a fool who will just give you money?

ref: and then they will hand him over to foreigners who will make fun of him. They will beat him and nail him to a cross. But on the third day he will rise from death. A Mother's Request (Mark
text: aŋ kpa ŋ we nala hʋ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma nosi tɩyaŋ. Ba sɩ yaa ŋ sɩya, a vɩɩrɩ ŋ, aŋ kpaasɩ ŋ mal daagarɩɩ tɩyaŋ. Amɛ ʋ kyɛtoo kyɛɛ Wɩɩsɩ sɩ kyisi ŋ sʋʋ tɩyaŋ.” Zebedi haaŋ sʋla Yesu kɩ kyɛ lɩhɔnɩya dɩ ʋ tɩya ʋ balɩya Maakɩ
translated: When I was going to Jerusalem, they wanted to kill me. They arrested me, they beat me, they put me in prison. But God's hand protected me." Zebedee's sons asked Jesus to let them sit at his right and left hand in his kingdom Mark

ref: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
text: Zebedi haaŋ gyɩ sii kaŋ ʋ biisi balɩya ko Yesu lee rɛ a tuu gbinni ʋ sɩya tɩyaŋ, aŋ sʋla ʋ dɩ ʋ pɛ ʋ bee wɩɩ kɩdɩgɩ.
translated: Zebedee's wife came with her sons to Jesus and knelt down to ask him for something.

ref: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
text: Doŋ nɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ma basɩ. Tʋwara hʋ ŋ aa sɩ na, ʋ tɩɩ rɛ ma mɛ sɩ na. Amɛ ŋ noduu abee ŋ nogɔbɔ hʋhɔnnɔ hʋ kɛ, ŋ daa sɩ lɩɩ ba. Ŋ kuwo Wɩɩsɩ marɩ doŋ nɛ a tɩya nala hʋ ʋ tɩɩ aa sɩ lɩɩ dɩ ba hɔŋ doŋ.”
translated: Then Jesus said to them, "Listen to me. Whoever receives me receives the one who sent me. And if you welcome a child in my name, you welcome me. You must know that God will say that whoever receives such a one has received him also."

ref: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.
text: Ɛɛ hal lɛ ba mʋ lɩɩ tɩŋ Gyɛriko tɩyaŋ kɩ mʋ, nɩgyamaa mɛ sii paa tɩŋ ba hal.
translated: And if they do not accept the Gyerike tradition with him, then we will also not accept their tradition.

ref: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
text: Beewɩya Gyɔɔŋ Wɩɩsɩnɩɩfool hʋ ko daga ma wɩtɩɩ ŋmanɩɩ hʋ rɛ ma aa sɩ tɩŋa, aŋka ma bɩ laa ʋ wɩya di, amɛ lampolaala abee gyaŋtɩrɩsɩ kɛ laa ʋ wɩya di rɛ. Abee ma aa naa gɛɛ hʋ ba aa laa ʋ wɩya di buloŋ, amɛ ma kɛ bɩ bɩrɩma ma hakɩllɩ a laa ʋ wɩya di.” Yesu maga baga tʋŋtʋnna badɔmɔŋ namaga Maakɩ
translated: Because the Kingdom of God is not something you can see with your eyes or point to, but rather it is something spiritual that comes into your heart. Those who truly enter it are not those who do it outwardly, but those who believe and accept it in their hearts." Jesus spoke these parables according to the Gospel of Mark

ref: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Ba aa maga nyɛ, dɩ baga hʋ tɩɩna rɛ ko, ɛɛ rɛ ʋ sɩ yaa baga tʋŋtʋnna no?”
translated: Then Jesus asked them, "They have not found him, so where did he say they should look for him?"

ref: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Farasiima sii lɩɩ a vʋʋlɩ gɛɛ hʋ ba aa sɩ magɩsɩ Yesu a bara kana ʋ abee wɩpɩyɛsɩya.
translated: And the Pharisees went out and held a council against Jesus how they might destroy him.

ref: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
text: Ba aa marɩ kɩdiiliye hʋ teŋ, ʋ tɩŋ ʋ tʋŋtʋnna dɩ ba mʋ yɩrɩ nala hʋ ʋ aa yɩra anɩɩ wɩɩ buloŋ yaa siri rɛ. Ba mʋ yɩrɩ ba, ba vɩya koyi.
translated: When a girl reaches puberty in the land, she and her age-mates who are in her compound or those she grew up with together go to the bush to dance. Those in her compound, they dance koyi.

ref: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
text: a baa, “Nɩhɩyawʋ, Wɩɩsɩ mɩrɩsɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ, ba kɩbee rɛ yaa ba buloŋ tɩyaŋ sɩfɩyaŋ wɩɩ?”
translated: "and said, 'Truly, I tell you, unless you change and become like little children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.'"

ref: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
text: “Ɛɛ rɛ ma bɩɩnɩ Krisita, laataal hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa lɩya wɩya? Kɩbee doho nal lɛ ʋ yaa?” Ɛɛ rɛ ba baa dɩ ʋ yaa Deviti nihii rɛ.
translated: "If you are the Christ, tell us plainly. How long will you keep us in suspense?" They said to him that it was the Feast of Dedication.

ref: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
text: Nal hʋ buloŋ aa kpa ʋ tɩɩ yohuŋ, Wɩɩsɩ sɩ kana ʋ hʋwɔlɩ, ka nal hʋ mɛ aa kaŋ ʋ tɩɩ hʋwɔlɩ, Wɩɩsɩ sɩ kpa ʋ yohuŋ.
translated: If someone loves the world and not God, God will leave him, but if someone loves God and not the world, God will love him.

ref: Whoever is the greatest should be the servant of the others.
text: Ʋ maga dɩ ma tɩyaŋ sɩlaal bɩrɩmɩ haŋbiye rɛ a tʋŋ tʋma kɩ tɩya ma buloŋ.
translated: The person who does not respect his/her parents will not live long on this earth.

ref: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
text: Ba aa yaa wɩɩ buloŋ a kɩ kyɛ dɩ nala na ba rɛ. Ba aa sabɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩya a we dagibiisi tɩyaŋ nɛ a kpa vʋwa ba gannɩ nosi tɩyaŋ abee ba nyupugini tɩyaŋ, aŋ leŋ ba gannɩ nyʋʋgyʋwasa mɛ doliye doliye a te ɛɛ hʋ nɩwaasɩma kɩna aa kɩya.
translated: They want to continue to do what they like. They have forgotten God's word which says that he will destroy their wealth and their property and their children, and also their wealth will be scattered in different places so that no one will know where it is.

ref: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
text: Mamaa nyʋlʋŋ-sɩlaala, ma sɩ na tʋwara! Ma aa baa, “Dɩ nal lɛ ŋmɩyɛsɩ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ, ʋ bɩ yaa ŋmɩyɛsʋʋ amɛ dɩ ʋ rɛ ko ŋmɩyɛsɩ salma kɩna hʋ aa we Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ, ʋ yaa ŋmɩyɛsʋʋ rɛ.”
translated: If someone says to you, do not believe it! If they say, "Look, here is the Christ," or "He is there," do not believe them, because false prophets will arise claiming to be the Christ, do not believe them.

ref: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
text: Ɛɛdɔŋ nyʋlʋŋbambugo! Salma kɩna hʋ aa we Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ abee dɩya hʋ aa leŋ salma hʋ bɩ kaŋ bisiŋ Wɩɩsɩ sɩya tɩyaŋ, kɩna no balɩya, kɩbee rɛ yaa sɩfɩyaŋ kɩŋ?
translated: What a wonderful thing! When a person does what God's word says and becomes the kind of person that God wants in his heart, what will happen, will he not be blessed?

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
text: Mamaa Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla abee Farasiima, ɛɛdɔŋ minaafigi tɩmma! Ma sɩ na tʋwara kɩŋkaŋ. Paaponni hʋ buloŋ ma aa kpa we kɩdiiliye tɩyaŋ ba sʋwala sʋma, ma aa kaŋ ba kpaa leye fi fi rɛ, a kpa lɩdɩgɩ tɩya Wɩɩsɩ. Ma aa yaa wɩmʋlla no buloŋ nɛ aŋ vɩya dɩ ma tɩŋ sɩfɩyaŋ wɩya hʋ Moosi fa aa biŋ tɩya ma anɩɩ ma kaŋ wɩtɩɩ wɩɩ buloŋ tɩyaŋ, a kɩ fá nala sikii, a kɩ tɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ abee wɩtɩɩ. Ma aa kaŋ ma kɩna kpaa leye fi fi, a kpa lɩdɩgɩ tɩya Wɩɩsɩ gɛɛ, ʋ yaa wɩwelii rɛ, amɛ ɛɛ bɩ daga dɩ ma vɩya wɩkaalɩya hʋ kɛ ta.
translated: I am a child of God through faith in Christ Jesus, not through works! I cannot boast. Because if I tried to keep all the commandments that our ancestors kept, and I failed to keep even one of them, I would be guilty before God. I would be like a person who is bound by chains that Moses gave us and we cannot break free from those chains, we cannot please God, we cannot serve God as children. If I cannot keep even one of them, I would be guilty before God also, He would judge me, but He has shown me His grace so that I may live.

ref: That's what you are like. Outside you look good, but inside you are evil and only pretend to be good.
text: Ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ ma mɛ kɩɩ nala sɩya tɩyaŋ. Dɩ ba aa rɛ na ma, ba aa bɩɩnɩ nɩɩ ma yaa nɩtɩɩma rɛ, ka ma tɩsɩ su abee wɩbɔmɔ abee minaafigi wɩya.
translated: If you do not give me food today, when they come to you, they will not give you water to drink, and you will die of thirst or hunger.

ref: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
text: Ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma rɛ, ma bɩl bɩ sɩ na ŋ, see saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ ma aa sɩ lɔllɩ ŋ a baa, ‘Nyusʋŋtɩɩna rɛ yaa nal hʋ aa ko Wɩɩsɩ feŋ tɩyaŋ.”’
translated: If you do not believe me, my children will testify for me, because they heard when I told their father, 'The one who will marry her is the one God chooses.'

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: Saŋa no tɩyaŋ dɩ nal buloŋ nɛ ko basɩ tɩya ɩ a baa, ‘Na Krisita hʋ we daha, koo ʋ we lee kɩdɩgɩ, ta laa ʋ tɩɩna di.’
translated: If anyone comes to you and does not bring this teaching, do not receive him into your house, and do not greet him.

ref: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
text: Ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ dɩ mamaa rɛ ko na wɩya no buloŋ aa yaa, ma gyɩma anɩɩ mɩyaŋ nihuwobiŋ Biye koyi mɛ kɔnɩ kpaga rɛ.
translated: And if our mother refuses to give us food today, I will report her to our father God when he returns in the evening.

ref: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
text: Saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye aa sɩ mɩɩgɩ ko, ʋ sɩ kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ wɩya hʋ gyɩ aa yaa saŋa hʋ Nowa gyɩ aa we ʋ mɩɩbol tɩyaŋ.
translated: When God saw that the wickedness of man was great on the earth, he decided to destroy all the people just as he had destroyed Noah's generation in the past.

ref: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
text: Ɛɛwɩya ma yaŋ gyɩŋ tɩya ma tɩɩ weliŋ, beewɩya ma bɩ gyɩŋ kyɛɛ hʋ ma Tɩɩna aa sɩ mɩɩgɩ ko.
translated: If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves and the Truth is not in us.

ref: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
text: Ma mɛ yaŋ yaa siri pɔ, beewɩya mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye sɩ mɩɩgɩ ko saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ ma aa bɩ yɩyɛla.” Tʋŋtʋnnɩ buloŋ maga ʋ kaŋ wɩtɩɩ rɛ Luka
translated: When I was with you in the temple daily, you did not lay hands on me." But this is your hour, and the power of darkness." The passage is from the Gospel of Luke

ref: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
text: Bambugo hʋ gyɩ bɩ lɔgɔ nʋʋ kolo, dɩ ba fɩŋtɩŋsɩ hʋ rɛ ko dɩsɩ dɩ ba fa kpa we.
translated: The bamboo is very strong and flexible, so they use it to make many things that they need.

ref: But the groom replied, “I don't even know you!”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ baa, “Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma, ŋ bɩ gyɩŋ ma.”
translated: He said to him, "The reason I came to you is that I am in trouble."

ref: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
text: Ʋ gyɩ pɩɩsa rɛ weliŋ ka ba nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ gyɩ na ko a yɩrɩ ba dɩ ʋ na ba kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ aa kpaa ʋ molbiye yaa gɛɛ.
translated: If someone does something good and they don't appreciate it, then that person will not do good things for them again or help them anymore.

ref: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ kambɩŋ kana ŋ, ŋ hul bʋwa a kpa molbiye hʋ a hogo. Na ɩ molbiye. Laa ɩ kɩna.’
translated: If you want to slaughter a goat, you must first tie its legs before you kill it. That is wisdom. That is knowledge.

ref: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
text: Ŋ gyɩ kaŋ teŋbil lɛ, ma kpa gal laalɩ ŋ. Ŋ gyɩ aa wɩɩlɩ lɛ, ma deŋ ŋ lee weliŋ. Ba gyɩ kana ŋ tɔ dɩyaŋ nɛ, ma ko na ŋ.”
translated: If you go to a funeral, I will go with you. If you are going to a wedding, I will accompany you there. They will not separate us from each other, I will die with you.

ref: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
text: Ŋ yaŋ sɩ baa, “Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma, saŋa hʋ buloŋ ma aa vɩya dɩ ma kyiyeli nala no tɩyaŋ nɩbiye, mɩyaŋ nɛ ma vɩya kyiyelii gɛɛ.
translated: I will say to him, "Friend, lend me three loaves of bread, because a friend of mine on a journey has come to me, and I have nothing to set before him."

ref: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”
text: Wɩɩsɩ sɩ leŋ dɩ nala no mʋ gyʋʋ lee hʋ ba aa sɩ na tʋwara a kaŋ kɩ mʋ deŋdeŋ, aŋka dɩ tɩpʋlʋŋ tɩmma hʋ mɛ kaŋ mɩɩbol hʋ aa bɩ tenii.”
translated: A person who has a field but does not cultivate it well so that it produces abundantly, but instead allows weeds to grow in it is not wise.

ref: Then they sang a hymn and went out to the Mount of Olives. Peter's Promise (Mark
text: Ɛɛ rɛ ba yɩɩ wɩɩkyʋwalyɩɩl aŋ sii lɩɩ a mʋ gyɩŋ Olivi dogimo hʋ. Yesu baa dɩ Piita sɩ tʋwa baa ʋ bɩ gyɩma ʋ Maakɩ
translated: And they went to a place called the Mount of Olives. Jesus had told Peter that before the rooster crowed he would deny him three times.

ref: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
text: Ba aa hɔnɔ kɩ di kɩna hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Yesu kpa paanʋʋ a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ aŋgyʋwasʋ aŋ kaŋ lɔgɔ lɔgɔ a kpa tɩya ʋ hatɩnna hʋ aŋ baa, “Ma laa kyaŋ. Ŋ teŋbii rɛ nyɛ.”
translated: And when they saw that he was sleeping in the boat, Jesus' disciples woke him up urgently and said to him, "Master, we are perishing. Don't you care about us?"

ref: This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven.
