text: Ka Yesu mɛ gyɩma ʋ tɩya tɩyaŋ anɩɩ dee kɩdɩgɩ lɩɩ ʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ. Ʋ bɩrɩmɩ nala hʋ lee a pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Aŋnɛ rɛ dige ŋ gal gɛɛ?”
translated: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, "Woman, here is your son."

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Biye hʋ nyagɩ sii a kɩ vala, nala hʋ buloŋ nyʋʋsɩ fɩyɛlɩ. Biye hʋ fa yaa bɩsɩ fi abee balɩya rɛ.
translated: If a child does not listen to advice, then that child will experience suffering. If a child refuses to listen, they will face consequences themselves.

ref: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ yaa a wuwo kɩ yaa wɩmagɩla no? Á gyɩma ʋ rɛ! Kapiŋta hʋ daa gɛɛ? Mɛɛrɩ biibaal daa gɛɛ. Gyemsi abee Gyoose abee Gyuda abee Siimɔŋ daa yaa ʋ naabalɩya? Ʋ naatoliye we daha.” Ɛɛ rɛ ba sii bee su ʋ.
translated: Is this not the carpenter's son? Is not his mother called Mary? And are not his brothers James and Joseph and Judas and Simon? And are not all his sisters with us?" So they took offense at him.

ref: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “A lɩɩ ma daatɩgɩya hal, ma ta bɩl kpaa kɩŋ buloŋ pɛ a vala ŋmanɩɩ hʋ. Ma ta kaŋ kɩdiiliye mʋ, a ta kpaa diiku mɛ kolo, a ta kpaa molbii mɛ.
translated: And when he had come to himself, he said, "How many of my father's hired servants have bread enough and to spare, but I perish here with hunger! I will arise and go to my father, and I will say to him, 'Father, I have sinned against heaven and before you.'

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: Kuwori hʋ aa nɩɩ wɩɩ no, ʋ tɩya buloŋ kyogi. Amɛ ʋ aa ŋmɩyɛsa nala hʋ buloŋ sɩɩ tɩyaŋ gɛɛ, ʋ bɩl bɩ sɩ wuwo lugisi ʋ nyʋwa.
translated: When a man marries a woman, he gives her a ring. But if the woman does not like the ring that was given, she can return it to the man her husband.

ref: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
text: Gyɔɔŋ hatɩnna hʋ aa nɩɩ wɩɩ no, ba sii ko a laa ʋ teŋbii a kaŋ mʋ hogo. Yesu tɩya nala tusi bʋnɔŋ kɩdiiliye ba di vɔgɔ Matiwu
translated: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, "Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?" When Jesus heard this, he said to them, "It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick." Matthew

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
text: Ba buloŋ di a vɔgɔ.
translated: They are coming to the farm.

ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: Ka ʋ yaŋ mʋ gyʋʋ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ. Ʋ aa gyʋʋ ko teŋ, puwo hʋ sige. Ʋ yaa ʋ hatɩnna hʋ wɩkperii.
translated: If you want to build a house, you must build it on the ground, not in the air. You must lay its foundation well.

ref: Their minds were closed, and they could not understand the true meaning of the loaves of bread. Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Matthew
text: Ba gyɩ naa dɩ Yesu kaŋ paanʋʋ gama bɔnɔŋ a kpaa nala tusi bʋnɔŋ nɛ, ka ba ha gyɩ bɩ gyɩŋ wɩɩ hʋ memii, beewɩya ba ha bɩ gyɩŋ nal hʋ Yesu aa yaa. Yesu tɩɩbɩ wɩɩla tɩmma Gyɛnɛsarɛtɩ tɩyaŋ Matiwu
translated: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret. And when the men of that place recognized him, they sent around to all that region and brought to him all who were sick, and implored him that they might only touch the fringe of his garment. And as many as touched it were made well. Then Jesus departed from there and went along the Sea of Galilee.

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Ba aa lɩɩ ko teŋ, dɩ nala gyɩma anɩɩ Yesu rɛ.
translated: When they arrived at the place, they crucified Jesus there.

ref: They noticed that some of his disciples ate without first washing their hands.
text: Ba aa ko ba naa dɩ Yesu hatɩnna hʋ badɔmɔŋ bɩ sɔŋ nɩɩ aŋ kɩ di kɩna. Nyɛ yaa bisiŋ nɛ ba lesiri tɩyaŋ.
translated: Those who did not believe in Jesus were saying that it was not true. But they were afraid of the Jewish leaders.

ref: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.
text: Nyɛ rɛ ʋ pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Ma ha bɩ kaŋ wɩgyʋŋ? Ma bɩ gyɩma anɩɩ dɩ nal lɛ di kɩŋ, ʋ bɩ sɩ wuwo leŋ dɩ ʋ kaŋ bisiŋ?
translated: And when he asked them, he said, "Why are you arguing? Why are you discussing among yourselves, is it because you don't have bread?"

ref: A woman whose daughter had an evil spirit in her heard where Jesus was. And at once she came and knelt down at his feet.
text: Haaŋ kɩdɩgɩ rɛ gyɩ we doŋ a lɩɩ Fɔniisɩya aa we Siiriya paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ, a yaa Giriki tɩɩna. Gyɩŋbɔŋ gyɩ kaŋ ʋ toluu.
translated: This man was from Phoenicia or from the region of Syria, and he spoke Greek. Therefore it was difficult for him.

ref: The woman was Greek and had been born in the part of Syria known as Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to force the demon out of her daughter.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ nɩɩ Yesu wɩya, a sii ko tuu tele ʋ naasɩ tɩyaŋ, a sʋla ʋ dɩ ʋ kile gyɩŋbɔŋ hʋ ta ʋ biye hʋ tɩyaŋ.
translated: When he heard that Jesus was coming, he went out to meet him on the road, and begged him to come to his house to heal his child.

ref: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
text: Yesu kana ʋ kaŋ lɩɩ nala hʋ tɩyaŋ. Ba dʋŋ balɩya hɔŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ kpa ʋ noniye a tʋʋ baal hʋ dɩgɩna tɩyaŋ aŋ tʋ naŋtʋwɔl dige ʋ naŋdeliŋ.
translated: Jesus did not want them to know where he was. He was teaching his disciples. He told them that the Son of Man would be handed over to people who would kill him, and after three days he would rise again.

ref: The Pharisees came out and started an argument with Jesus. They wanted to test him by asking for a sign from heaven.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Farasiima badɔmɔŋ sii ko Yesu lee, a kɩ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ wɩya. Ba gyɩ aa kyɛ dɩ ba magɩsɩ ʋ na wɩya, ba baa dɩ ʋ yaa wɩmagɩl dɩ ba na a gyɩma anɩɩ ʋ dee hʋ lɩɩ Wɩɩsɩ lee rɛ.
translated: When the Pharisees heard what Jesus said, they did not like it. They wanted to find a way to arrest him, but they were afraid because the people believed that he was a prophet sent by God.

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?” “You are the Messiah!” Peter replied.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl pɩyɛsɩ ʋ hatɩnna hʋ mɛ a baa, “Ka ma mɛ, ma baa ŋ yaa aŋnɛ rɛ?” Piita baa “Ɩ rɛ yaa Krisita hʋ, laataal hʋ Wɩɩsɩ a lɩya.”
translated: And when Jesus asked his disciples what they say about him, Peter said "You are the Christ, the Son of the living God."

ref: Jesus warned the disciples not to tell anyone about him. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Matthew
text: Piita a basɩ gɛɛ rɛ, Yesu basɩ tɩya ba abee sɩfɩyasɩ dɩ ba pɔ dɩ ba ta ko basɩ wɩɩ no tɩya nal buloŋ. Yesu basa ɛɛ hʋ ʋ aa sɩ na tʋwara aŋ sʋba Matiwu
translated: After Peter said that, Jesus began to tell his disciples that he must go to Jerusalem and suffer many things. Jesus said this to prepare them for what was going to happen to Matthew

ref: Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “The nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make the Son of Man suffer terribly. He will be rejected and killed, but three days later he will rise to life.”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu piili kɩ daga ʋ hatɩnna hʋ a baa, “Mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ biye, ŋ sɩ na tʋwara kɩŋkaŋ. Bee nɩhɩyasɩ abee wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ, abee Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla, ba buloŋ sɩ vɩya ŋ aŋ leŋ dɩ ba kpʋ ŋ. Dɩ ba aa rɛ ko kpʋ ŋ, ŋ sɩ yaa kyɛyɛ boto aŋ mɩɩgɩ sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ.”
translated: When Jesus finished saying these things to them, he said, "Children of Nineveh, I will not leave you orphans. Whether you are in trouble or in joy, whether the Holy Spirit is teaching you, they will not be able to take me away from you or kill me. If they do not kill me, I will come again and stay with you forever."

ref: Jesus then told the crowd and the disciples to come closer, and he said: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
text: Nyɛ rɛ Yesu yɩrɩ ʋ hatɩnna hʋ abee nala hʋ buloŋ ba ko, ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Dɩ nal buloŋ nɛ aa kyɛ dɩ ʋ tɩŋa ŋ, see dɩ ʋ vɩya ʋ tɩɩ ŋmanɩɩ aŋ kpa ʋ daagarɩɩ baasɩ, a tɩŋa ŋ.
translated: When Jesus saw that a crowd was running together to him, he rebuked the spirit, saying to it, "You deaf and mute spirit, I command you to come out of him and never enter him again."

ref: I can assure you that Elijah has already come. And people treated him just as they wanted to, as the Scriptures say they would. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
text: Lagɩlagɩ no, ŋ sɩ basɩ tɩya ma anɩɩ Ilaagya paalɩ ko rɛ. Ba kana ʋ yaa ʋ gɛɛ hʋ ba aa kyɛ anɩɩ ba aa saba gɛɛ Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ tɩyaŋ.” Yesu tɩɩbɩ biye gyɩŋbɔŋ aa kana Matiwu
translated: Therefore, if you do these things, you will never stumble. For in this way, entry into the eternal kingdom of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ will be richly provided for you." Jesus chose Matthew who was a tax collector

ref: After Jesus and the disciples had gone back home and were alone, they asked him, “Why couldn't we force out that demon?”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bee ʋ hatɩnna hʋ mʋ gyʋʋ dɩya. Ba lʋwɔlɩ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Bee rɛ tɩŋ á kɛ bɩ wuwo kil gyɩŋbɔŋ hʋ?”
translated: Then Jesus asked his disciples this question. They answered him saying, "Who do people say that I am?"

ref: So they kept it to themselves. But they wondered what he meant by the words “raised from death.”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ ba laa wɩɩ hʋ we ba nyuni tɩyaŋ, aŋ kɩ pɩyɛsɩ dɔmɔŋ, “Ʋ aa sɩ sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ, ʋ memii rɛ yaa bee?”
translated: When they saw that he was not afraid at all, they were very surprised, saying, "You are not afraid at all, what kind of person are you?"

ref: “When you welcome even a child because of me, you welcome me. And when you welcome me, you welcome the one who sent me.” For or against Jesus (Luke
text: “Dɩ nal lɛ kuu ŋ wɩya a kaŋ biibiye no dɔŋtɩɩna weliŋ, mɩyaŋ nɛ ʋ kaŋ weliŋ gɛɛ. Nal hʋ mɛ buloŋ aa kana ŋ weliŋ, ŋ dʋŋ daa ʋ kaŋ weliŋ. Nal hʋ aa tɩma ŋ mɛ rɛ ʋ kaŋ weliŋ gɛɛ.” Baal kɩdɩgɩ kile gyɩŋbɔmɔ ta Yesu feŋ tɩyaŋ Luka
translated: If someone forces you to carry their load for one mile, carry it for two miles. Give to anyone who asks you for something, and do not turn away from anyone who wants to borrow from you." These are some of the teachings Jesus gave according to Luke

ref: At once the boy's father shouted, “I do have faith! Please help me to have even more.”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ biye hʋ kuwo sii sɩŋ a heeli basɩ a baa, “Ŋ laa ɩ di rɛ, amɛ ʋ bɩ maga. Pɛ ŋ tɩyaŋ dɩ ŋ laa ɩ di yʋga.”
translated: And when the child refused to eat, he told his father saying, "I will not eat it, but you should keep it. So that when I am hungry I will eat it later."

ref: Jesus answered, “Only prayer can force out this kind of demon.” Jesus Again Speaks about His Death (Matthew
text: Ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Nal buloŋ bɩ sɩ wuwo kile gyɩŋbɔŋ no iriŋ, see ʋ aa sʋla Wɩɩsɩ weliŋ.” Yesu bɩl basɩ ʋ sʋʋ wɩya Matiwu
translated: And they said to him, "John's disciples fast often and make prayers, and likewise the disciples of the Pharisees, but yours eat and drink." Jesus answered them

ref: Jesus left with his disciples and started through Galilee. He did not want anyone to know about it,
text: Ɛɛ rɛ ba sii lɩɩ doŋ, a tuu tɩŋ Galili paalʋʋ mʋ. Yesu fa bee kyɛ dɩ nal buloŋ gyɩŋ lee hʋ ʋ aa we.
translated: When they had finished eating, they returned to Galilee. Jesus had not yet revealed himself to the disciples in this way before.

ref: If your foot causes you to sin, chop it off. You would be better off to go into life lame than to have two feet and be thrown into hell.
text: Dɩ ɩ naaŋ nɛ aa kyɛ dɩ ʋ kana ɩ we wɩbɔmɔ yayɩ tɩyaŋ, keri ta. Ɩ aa sɩ mʋ wɩɩsɩbee abee naakuu, ʋ rɛ kpɩya ba aa sɩ kpa ɩ yuwo we diŋ tɩyaŋ abee naapilime. ‘Lee hʋ kyaaŋkyʋwana aa sɩ di ɩ teŋbii deŋdeŋ a bɩ sɩ sʋba, ka diŋ hʋ mɛ bɩ sɩ dɩsɩ.’
translated: If you want him to do your work well, don't do it. You should give him two days, he will finish it and give you your thing in three days. 'Because if you stand over him while he is working, he will not be happy, and the thing too will not be good.'

ref: After Jesus sat down and told the twelve disciples to gather around him, he said, “If you want the place of honor, you must become a slave and serve others!”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu hɔŋ aŋ yɩrɩ ʋ hatɩnna fi abee balɩya hʋ, a basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Nal hʋ aa kyɛ dɩ ʋ yaa ma tɩyaŋ nɩbal, see dɩ ʋ kaŋ ʋ tɩɩ bɩrɩmɩ ma tɩyaŋ haŋbiye a tʋma tɩya ma buloŋ.”
translated: When Jesus saw that his teaching had attracted many people, he said to them, "Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross and follow me."

ref: Salt is good. But if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made salty again? Have salt among you and live at peace with each other.
text: Yɩsɩ sʋma, amɛ dɩ ʋ sʋmɩŋ ko lɩɩ, ɩ bɩl bɩ sɩ wuwo yaa ʋ dɩ ʋ sʋma. Ma leŋ dɩ ma tɩsɩ sʋma anɩɩ yɩsɩ aa sʋma gɛɛ, aŋ leŋ dɩ ma buloŋ kaŋ nyʋʋdɩgɩ.”
translated: If someone, when he is dying, says that his children should not bury him in his home. But I and my younger brother, when he died, we buried him at home.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: Don't stop him! No one who works miracles in my name will soon turn and say something bad about me.
text: Amɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Dɩ ʋ rɛ aa yaa gɛɛ, ma ta kile ʋ. Dɩ nal lɛ yaa wɩmagɩl buloŋ ŋ feŋ tɩyaŋ, ʋ kyiye bɩl bɩ sɩ wuwo kyogi ŋ feŋ.
translated: Then Jesus said to them, "If you love me, you will obey me. If someone loves their child and the child asks for something, they give what is good for the child.

ref: So if your hand causes you to sin, cut it off! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed than to have two hands and be thrown into the fires of hell that never go out.
text: Dɩ ɩ noŋ nɛ aa kyɛ dɩ ʋ kana ɩ we wɩbɔmɔ yayɩ tɩyaŋ, keri ta. Ɩ aa sɩ mʋ wɩɩsɩbee abee nokuu, ʋ rɛ kpɩya ɩ aa kaŋ nopilime aŋ gyʋʋ diŋ, ‘Lee hʋ kyaaŋkyʋwana aa sɩ di ɩ teŋbii deŋdeŋ a bɩ sɩ sʋba, ka diŋ hʋ mɛ bɩ sɩ dɩsɩ.’
translated: If you want him to give you something good, don't be shy. When you ask him for bread, he won't give you a stone instead, saying 'This hard thing is what I have in my house that I can give, I don't have anything else to give.'
